title: An All Time Low
speaker: Pastor Tito Lyro
source_files: 04-06-2025 -AM- An All Time Low - Hosea 4 - Pastor Tito Lyro.txt

outline.title: Context: The Shift in Hosea Chapter 4
subpoints: Chapters 1-3 used the veil of Hosea’s marriage to Gomer as a symbol of God’s relationship to Israel
In chapter 4 the veil is removed: God speaks directly to Israel through the prophet
Hosea is no longer in the first person; Gomer is out of the picture
Chapter 4 represents the lowest point in Israel’s walk with God in the entire book
This passage is best understood as directed to the visible church, not merely a foreign nation

outline.title: God’s Threefold Charge Against Israel (vv. 1-3)
subpoints: No truth (trustworthiness and reliability): Israel is not a people you can count on
No mercy (covenant faithfulness, steadfast love): the Hebrew hesed—the love and loyalty expected of covenant partners
No knowledge of God: not merely ignorance but willful rejection of God
The evidence: swearing, lying, murder, stealing, adultery, bloodshed upon bloodshed
Consequence: the land mourns; all creation suffers (v. 3; cf. Romans 8)

outline.title: How Did Israel Get Here? Two Historical Events
subpoints: General principle: the hearts of men are wicked; social entropy naturally leads to disorder without fresh input
First event: Solomon’s backsliding (1 Kings 11). He married 700 wives and 300 concubines from pagan nations; his heart was turned to other gods
God had appeared to Solomon twice and forbidden this; yet Solomon’s obedience to his wives led to idolatry affecting the entire kingdom
Second event: Jeroboam’s idolatry (1 Kings 12:25-33). He made two golden calves, appointed non-Levitical priests, changed the feast dates, and made worship convenient
Jeroboam’s strategy: “If I allow people to worship God rightly, I’m done.” He gave the people what they wanted

outline.title: Lessons for the Church: Reformed and Always Reforming
subpoints: Israel’s history is a lesson for us (1 Corinthians 10:11; Romans 15:4)
The solution to drift is the Word of God held in the hands of the Spirit
Three things the Lord requires to avoid becoming Israel: look to the testimony of faithful people; remove whatever keeps you from following the Lord; look to Jesus
Hebrews 12:1-2: lay aside every weight and run the race looking to Jesus, the author and finisher of faith
Application: we are no better than Israel; our hearts are the same. But God sent Jesus to the cross for the joy of saving such people as us

summary:
Pastor Tito Lyro continues the Hosea series with a sermon on chapter 4, titled “An All Time Low.” He establishes that Hosea 4 marks the absolute nadir of Israel’s spiritual condition in the book—the point at which God removes all metaphorical distance and speaks directly to his people. The critical interpretive move of the sermon is to read the passage not as a description of a foreign nation’s moral failure, but as a charge against the visible church.

God’s indictment in verses 1-3 is threefold: no truth (covenant reliability), no mercy (steadfast love, hesed), and no knowledge of God. This last charge is the thread that runs through the entire book, reaching its famous summary in Hosea 6:6. Pastor Lyro argues that the evidence God cites in verse 2 (swearing, lying, murder, theft, adultery) shows these charges are not empty accusations but documented facts. Even creation itself mourns as a result (v. 3; cf. Romans 8).

The bulk of the sermon investigates the question: How did Israel reach this all-time low? Two historical events are identified as the primary causes. First, Solomon’s backsliding (1 Kings 11): his marriage to 700 pagan wives turned his heart to other gods despite two divine appearances warning him not to do this. His sin affected the entire nation for generations. Second, Jeroboam’s idolatry (1 Kings 12): when he made two golden calves and restructured Israelite worship for his own political purposes, he intuitively understood that true worship of God empowers God’s people to resist human authority. He replaced it with convenient, people-pleasing counterfeits.

The sermon closes by drawing three lessons for the contemporary church from Israel’s history (1 Corinthians 10:11): look to the testimony of faithful saints, remove whatever keeps you from following the Lord, and above all look to Jesus—who endured the cross for the joy of saving a people whose hearts are no different from Israel’s in Hosea 4.
